title: medicago sativa subsp. vulgaris
intro: Medicago sativa subsp. vulgaris, commonly known as common alfalfa or lucerne, is a subspecies of the legume plant Medicago sativa, native to Central Asia and Southern Europe. It is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Medicago sativa subsp. vulgaris include reducing cholesterol levels, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for cardiovascular health and autoimmune diseases. Herbal preparations of Medicago sativa subsp. vulgaris include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
